display.purpose: Screen headings, book titles on the Home shelf.

title.purpose: Section headings, modal titles, flashcard prompts.

section-title.purpose: List group headers, sheet panel headings.

body.purpose: All UI body text — descriptions, labels, definition prose.

label.purpose: Buttons, chips, tab labels, metadata pairs.

caption.purpose: Secondary metadata — page counts, timestamps, stat chip sub-labels.

eyebrow.purpose: Structural section dividers. Maximum one per screen section.

shadows.name: card-lift
shadows.purpose: Applied to elevated card containers that need to read as interactive or raised. Not at rest in list contexts.

shadows.name: subtle-lift
shadows.purpose: Modal sheet handles, floating action buttons, lightweight overlay surfaces.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions (opacity, color, scale).

motion.name: ease-decelerate
motion.purpose: Elements entering the screen (sheets sliding up, panels expanding).

motion.name: duration-short
motion.purpose: Micro-interactions: opacity on press, color on state change.

components.name: Card — Elevated
components.description: Primary content container. Carries the card-lift shadow. Never nest inside another elevated card.
components.html: Book title or featured content goes here.
components.css: .ds-card-elevated { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #ddd5c8; border-radius: 22px; padding: 20px; box-shadow: 0 10px 24px rgba(41,28,14,0.08); font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 15px; line-height: 22px; color: #1a1a1a; margin: 0; }

components.name: Card — Muted
components.description: Inset secondary container. Definition panels, expandable detail sections. No shadow.
components.html: Secondary content or definition panel.
components.css: .ds-card-muted { background: #f0ece4; border-radius: 22px; padding: 20px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 15px; line-height: 22px; color: #1a1a1a; margin: 0; }

components.name: Icon Button — Neutral
components.description: Default action button. Pill shape, 1px border. For non-primary actions: share, filter, edit.
components.css: .ds-icon-btn-neutral { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: #fcfbf7; border: 1px solid #ddd5c8; border-radius: 999px; padding: 12px 16px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 18px; color: #1a1a1a; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-icon-btn-neutral:hover { opacity: 0.82; } .ds-icon-btn-neutral:active { opacity: 0.7; } .ds-icon-btn-neutral:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #b8552e; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Icon Button — Accent
components.description: Selected or contextually active state. Terra cotta soft wash signals primary interaction.
components.css: .ds-icon-btn-accent { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; background: #f5e8e2; border: none; border-radius: 999px; padding: 12px 16px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 18px; color: #1a1a1a; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-icon-btn-accent:hover { opacity: 0.82; } .ds-icon-btn-accent:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #b8552e; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Stat Chip — Accent
components.description: Highlighted stat display: a numeric value above a text label. Vocabulary counts, primary metrics.
components.css: .ds-stat-chip-accent { display: inline-flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; min-width: 86px; background: #f5e8e2; border-radius: 16px; padding: 12px 16px; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-stat-value { font-size: 20px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 26px; color: #1a1a1a; letter-spacing: 0.1px; } .ds-stat-label { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 16px; color: #978e81; letter-spacing: 0.2px; }

components.name: Tab Bar Item
components.description: Bottom navigation tab. Active state in terra cotta. Idle in warm gray. Icon above label.
components.css: .ds-tab-bar { display: flex; height: 72px; background: #faf6ee; border-top: 1px solid #e4dac6; padding: 9px 0 12px; } .ds-tab-item { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 4px; flex: 1; background: none; border: none; color: #b3a892; font-family: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 16px; cursor: pointer; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-tab-item--active { color: #b8552e; } .ds-tab-item:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #b8552e; outline-offset: -2px; }

narrative.overview:
Fluent Fable is the experience of reading a beloved book with a knowledgeable friend nearby — someone who can instantly surface a word's etymology, break apart a character into its radicals, or translate a tricky sentence, then vanish so you can keep reading. The design carries this idea into every decision: the text is primary, the tooling is secondary, and depth arrives only when summoned.

The visual language is warm but precise. Not the warm of a café (soft, unfocused, inviting drift) but the warm of a reading lamp on a clear desk: contained heat, clear shadow edges, a surface that has been chosen and arranged. Surfaces layer in tone — from the warm neutral page to subtly tinted containers to crisp elevated cards — without resorting to heavy shadow or decorative flourish. The palette's terra cotta accent is the only moment of color saturation; it carries the weight of interactive elements the way a red pencil marks a manuscript.

This system explicitly rejects: the gamified reward loops of Duolingo and Quizlet (no streaks, badges, confetti, or cartoon characters), the clinical monotony of Anki (no default-gray grids that feel like homework), and the anonymous warmth of Kindle's sepia default (which has a point of view but not a voice).
keyCharacteristics: Text-first hierarchy — UI chrome pulls back, content leads
Depth on demand — rich information layers surface only when the user asks
Warm neutrals grounded by a single terra cotta accent, not a spectrum of color
Earned elevation — shadows appear in response to state, not as ambient decoration
Bilingual typographic capability — Fraunces + DM Sans for Latin scripts; Noto Serif KR for Korean

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Terra Cotta (#b8552e) is the only saturated hue in the system. It appears on interactive elements and active states only.

rules.name: The Warm-Not-Generic Rule
rules.body: The neutral ground is warm because books are warm, not because 'warm feels premium.' Do not extend this warmth to decorative gradient overlays or tinted backgrounds behind hero text.

rules.name: The Serif-for-Content Rule
rules.body: Fraunces is for content hierarchy and reader-facing moments. DM Sans carries all functional UI. The two fonts do not appear in the same visual role on the same screen.

rules.name: The Reader Font Rule
rules.body: Font choice and size in the ebook reading view is user-controlled. Design system typography governs the UI shell, not the page content.

rules.name: The Flat-First Rule
rules.body: A surface has no shadow at rest. Shadow is a response to state (pressed, elevated, presented). If you're adding a shadow to make something 'feel like a card,' use tonal background stepping instead.

dos: Do let reading content own the screen. UI chrome stays at the periphery.
Do use terra cotta (#b8552e) exclusively for interactive and active states. Its scarcity is its signal.
Do use tonal surface stepping (background-warm → surface → surface-elevated) to create depth before reaching for shadows.
Do use Fraunces for content hierarchy and DM Sans for functional UI. Keep the pairing clean.
Do size and render CJK text at appropriate line heights; 22pt is minimum for comfortable Hangul/Hanzi reading.
Do respect the user's chosen font and size in the reader — design system typography governs the shell, not the page.
Do earn shadows with state. Shadows appear on interaction, not at rest.
donts: Don't add gamification UI — streaks, confetti, mascot characters, or score badges.
Don't use clinical card grids styled like Anki or Quizlet.
Don't reproduce Duolingo's color saturation or playful illustration style.
Don't use a second accent color. The accent is terra cotta and only terra cotta.
Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored stripe on cards, callouts, or list items.
Don't apply gradient text (background-clip: text). Emphasis via weight or size, not visual effect.
Don't nest elevated cards inside elevated cards.
Don't use eyebrow labels on every section. Maximum one per screen section.
Don't use orange-amber (#c87d00) as the primary accent — the system has been unified to terra cotta.
